U
Oracle Events
1. Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations) or airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations) to retrieve all records. Returns 63 total records including 26 In Progress. Active moves by coordinator: Javier Morales has 8 (including Rachel Dominguez Apr 7-9, Sasha Greenwald Apr 18-20, Jordan Whitfield Apr 21-23), Suki Patel has 8 (including Daniel Reeves Apr 10-12, Maya Johansson Apr 14-16, Andre Mitchell Apr 22-24), Fatimah Al-Rashidi has 5 (including Tomás Herrera Apr 14-16, Lena Kowalski Apr 22-24), Blessing Okafor has 5 (including Kevin Okafor Apr 14-16, Preethi Nair Apr 21-23). Javier and Suki are the most overloaded. Among Javier's moves, Jordan Whitfield (Apr 21-23, economy flight, no special handling) has the most flexible timeline. Among Suki's moves, Andre Mitchell (Apr 22-24, mechanical prototyping workspace, no hazmat) has the most flexible timeline. Neither has hard client deadlines or hazmat handling. The most time-sensitive moves are Simone Richter (Apr 4-6, URGENT - lease ends April 6) and Rachel Dominguez (Apr 7-9).
2. Update Airtable record for Jordan Whitfield using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, records: [{id: recReloc00000000035, fields: {Assigned Coordinator: "Blessing Okafor"}}]). Javier goes from 8 to 7 active moves, Blessing from 5 to 6.
3. Update Airtable record for Andre Mitchell using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, records: [{id: recReloc00000000038, fields: {Assigned Coordinator: "Fatimah Al-Rashidi"}}]). Suki goes from 8 to 7 active moves, Fatimah from 5 to 6. Final distribution: Javier 7, Suki 7, Fatimah 6, Blessing 6.
4. Search Linear for tickets related to Jordan Whitfield and Andre Mitchell using linear_list_issues. No existing relocation tracking tickets are found for either employee. If relocation tracking tickets were found, add a comment using linear_create_comment explaining the coordinator reassignment and the reason (workload rebalancing).
5. Search for the #operations Slack channel using channels_list. Find channel C006 ("operations").
6. Post a summary in #operations using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C006) explaining: current coordinator loads, the two reassignments made (Jordan Whitfield from Javier to Blessing, Andre Mitchell from Suki to Fatimah), new load distribution, and that no hazmat or hard-deadline moves were touched.
7. Look up coordinator email addresses using contacts_search_contacts (queries: "Javier Morales", "Suki Patel", "Fatimah Al-Rashidi", "Blessing Okafor"). Find javier.morales@moveops.com, suki.patel@moveops.com, fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, blessing.okafor@moveops.com.
8. Send emails to each affected coordinator using send_email (sender: chloe.vance@moveops.com) - notify Javier and Suki that a move was removed from their queue (with the specific relocation and reason), and notify Fatimah and Blessing that a move was added (with the employee name, route, timeline, and relevant details).
